CCN: 395684 · CABOT, PA 16023 · Butler County

Overview

Address
134 MARWOOD ROAD, CABOT, PA 16023
Phone
7243521571
Certified beds
142
Avg daily residents
136 (95% of beds filled)
Ownership
Non-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1984-12-01
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.44 4.09 3.24 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.82 0.72 0.78 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.78 0.91 0.74 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.83 2.47 1.73 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.60 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.12 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.10
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.62
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.93
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
32.9%
annual
RN turnover
20.8%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.45
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.05
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-08-08 12 12 1 68 1 68
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-08-29 14 10 4 143 1 143
Total weighted health score
86.8
lower is better
Infection-control citations
0
last cycle

Deficiencies (34)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
32 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-08-08 2025-09-15
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2025-08-08 2025-09-15
0575 Post a list of names, addresses, and telephone numbers of all pertinent State agencies and advocacy groups and a statement that the resident may file a complaint with the State Survey Agency. D 2025-08-08 2025-09-15
0605 Prevent the use of unnecessary psychotropic medications or use medications that may restrain a resident's ability to function. D 2025-08-08 2025-09-15
0628 Provide the required documentation or notification related to the resident's needs, appeal rights, or bed-hold policies. E 2025-08-08 2025-09-15
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-08-08 2025-09-15
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-08-08 2025-09-15
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2025-08-08 2025-09-15
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-08-08 2025-09-15
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-08-08 2025-09-15
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. D 2025-08-08 2025-09-15
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-08-08 2025-09-15
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. C 2024-09-12 2024-10-14
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. E 2024-08-29 2024-10-14
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-08-29 2024-10-14
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-08-29 2024-10-14
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-08-29 2024-10-14
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. E 2024-08-29 2024-10-14
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-08-29 2024-10-14
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2024-08-29 2024-10-14
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-08-29 2024-10-14
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-08-29 2024-10-14
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-08-29 2024-10-14
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2024-07-12 2024-07-24
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. D 2024-07-12 2024-07-24
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2023-10-20 2023-11-24
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-10-20 2023-11-24
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-10-20 2023-11-24
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2023-10-20 2023-11-24
0728 Ensure that nurse aides who have worked more than 4 months, are trained and competent; and nurse aides who have worked less than 4 months are enrolled in appropriate training. D 2023-10-20 2023-11-24
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. D 2023-10-20 2023-11-24
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2023-10-20 2023-11-24
0947 Ensure nurse aides have the skills they need to care for residents, and give nurse aides education in dementia care and abuse prevention. D 2023-10-20 2023-11-24
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2023-07-13 2023-08-07
